basic_details.job_title: District Welfare Office Dumka Part-time/Period-based Teachers Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 83 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: District Magistrate-cum-Deputy Commissioner Office, Dumka (Welfare Branch) / District Welfare Office, Dumka (ITDA Dumka)
basic_details.post_name: Part-time/Period-based Teachers (Ghanti Adharit Shikshak) - Primary, Subject and +2 Level Teachers
basic_details.short_information: Dumka Ghanti Adharit Shikshak Recruitment 2026 notification has been released by the District Welfare Office, Dumka (ITDA Dumka) for 83 Part-time/Period-based Teachers posts under the Service Procurement mode in ST and Backward Class residential schools of Dumka district. This is the Notification Released / Application Open stage recruitment, with applications accepted offline from 04-07-2026 to 25-07-2026 (up to 5:00 PM). This notification covers multiple teaching posts and subjects under a single advertisement (Advertisement No. 1062).

application_fee.details: Not Announced Yet. The official notification does not mention any application fee details.

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 21 years Maximum Age: 55 years Age is calculated as on 31-05-2026 | Age Relaxation: Not Announced Yet.

qualification.details: Only candidates who are domiciles of Dumka (home) district are eligible to apply. Applicants must attach a residential certificate issued by a competent officer of Dumka district.
qualification.education_qualification: Graduate in the related subject + B.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be paid Rs. 200 per teaching period (per ghanti), with a maximum of 4 periods per working day and 5 working days per week. No other allowance is admissible, and payment is based on daily teaching attendance.

selection_process: Selection will be made purely on a temporary basis by a district-level committee based on merit (मेधाक्रम) among PG/Graduate/B.Ed or teacher-training degree holders. Preference will be given to candidates of the respective block
and local candidates of the area where the residential school is located. If eligible candidates are not available at the block level

eligible candidates available at the district level will be selected. All selections will follow the Jharkhand government's district reservation roster provisions applicable to direct recruitment for district-level posts. No written examination details are mentioned in the notification.
how_to_apply: Download the prescribed application form from the official website: www.dumka.nic.in.
Fill in all details in block/capital letters as instructed in the form.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents mentioned above.
Clearly write the subject name applied for on top of the sealed envelope.

Submit the sealed envelope by post or in person to: Project Director, Integrated Tribal Development Agency (ITDA), Welfare Branch, Dumka, Pin – 814101 (Collectorate, Dumka Block-A), within office hours.
Ensure the application reaches the office on or before 25-07-2026, 5:00 PM.
important_instructions: Applications will be accepted only in the prescribed format; incomplete applications or those in any other format will not be accepted. Applications received after the last date will not be accepted. One candidate can submit only one application. This engagement is purely temporary and alternative; no claim for regularization or permanency will be entertained in future. Selected teachers will initially be engaged for a maximum of 6 months, extendable by another 6 months on satisfactory service, subject to a maximum total tenure of 1 year, or until permanent teachers are posted by the department, whichever is earlier. No leave or transfer benefits will be admissible; no residential facility will be provided at the school. Service can be terminated immediately without prior notice for violation of agreement terms or unsatisfactory work.

content_sections.heading: What is Dumka Ghanti Adharit Shikshak Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Dumka Ghanti Adharit Shikshak Recruitment 2026 is a temporary, hourly-based teacher hiring drive by the District Welfare Office, Dumka, for 83 posts across primary and subject-teacher categories in 15 ST/Backward Class residential schools. Selection is purely temporary and alternative in nature, based on merit, for a maximum tenure of one year. Applications must be submitted offline in sealed envelopes by 25-07-2026.

content_sections.heading: Quick Highlights
content_sections.text: Organization: District Welfare Office, Dumka (ITDA Dumka) Post Name: Part-time/Period-based Teachers (Ghanti Adharit Shikshak) - multiple subjects Total Vacancies: 83 Apply Mode: Offline (sealed envelope submission) Start Date: 04-07-2026 Last Date: 25-07-2026 (up to 5:00 PM) Qualification: B.Ed/Graduate/Postgraduate with B.Ed as per post (see qualification table below) Age Limit: 21 to 55 years (as on 31-05-2026)

content_sections.heading: Notification PDF and Official Website
content_sections.text: Download District Welfare Office, Dumka Part-time/Period-based Teachers Recruitment PDF

content_sections.heading: District Welfare Office Dumka Part-time/Period-based Teachers Recruitment 2026 Overview

rows.district_magistrate_cum_deputy_commissio: Part-time/Period-based Teachers (Ghanti Adharit Shikshak) - Primary, Subject and +2 Level Teachers

content_sections.heading: District Welfare Office Dumka Part-time/Period-based Teachers Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 83 posts are available across primary-level and subject-teacher categories for ST/Backward Class residential schools in Dumka district. Category-wise vacancy break-up: General (GEN) – 37, EWS – 05, ST – 37, SC – 04. Vacancies are placed across 15 residential schools in Dumka district, based on posting/vacancy details:

rows.school_name: +2 Backward Class Residential Girls High School, Kuruwa
ST Residential Girls High School, Nakati
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Kairabani
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Lakhanpur
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Gummapahari
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Manjhiyada
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Shikaripada
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Masalia
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Jarmundi
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Aasanpahari
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Upar Murgathali
ST Residential Boys Upgraded High School, Aasansol

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Only candidates who are domiciles of Dumka (home) district are eligible to apply. Applicants must attach a residential certificate issued by a competent officer of Dumka district.

rows.post_category: Social Science, Science, Sanskrit, English, Maths, Hindi, Music/Physical Education (Sl. No. 2–8)
rows.minimum_qualification: Graduate in the related subject + B.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution

rows.post_category: Physics, Biology, Chemistry, Maths for +2 schools (Sl. No. 9–12)
rows.minimum_qualification: Postgraduate in the related subject + B.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Minimum Age: 21 years Maximum Age: 55 years Age is calculated as on 31-05-2026 Age Relaxation: Not Announced Yet.

content_sections.heading: Salary / Honorarium
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be paid Rs. 200 per teaching period (per ghanti), with a maximum of 4 periods per working day and 5 working days per week. No other allowance is admissible, and payment is based on daily teaching attendance.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Not Announced Yet. The official notification does not mention any application fee details.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection will be made purely on a temporary basis by a district-level committee based on merit (मेधाक्रम) among PG/Graduate/B.Ed or teacher-training degree holders. Preference will be given to candidates of the respective block, local language speakers, and local candidates of the area where the residential school is located. If eligible candidates are not available at the block level, eligible candidates available at the district level will be selected. All selections will follow the Jharkhand government's district reservation roster provisions applicable to direct recruitment for district-level posts. No written examination details are mentioned in the notification.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Duly filled application form (in the prescribed format only) Recent passport-size colour photograph Self-attested copies of all educational/academic certificates as per the qualification table in the application Aadhaar Card Caste certificate (photocopy, for ST/SC/EWS candidates) issued by a competent officer of Dumka district Residential/domicile certificate issued by a competent officer of Dumka district Technical qualification certificates, if applicable

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for District Welfare Office Dumka Part-time/Period-based Teachers
content_sections.text: Download the prescribed application form from the official website: www.dumka.nic.in. Fill in all details in block/capital letters as instructed in the form. Attach self-attested copies of all required documents mentioned above. Clearly write the subject name applied for on top of the sealed envelope. Submit the sealed envelope by post or in person to: Project Director, Integrated Tribal Development Agency (ITDA), Welfare Branch, Dumka, Pin – 814101 (Collectorate, Dumka Block-A), within office hours. Ensure the application reaches the office on or before 25-07-2026, 5:00 PM.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Applications will be accepted only in the prescribed format; incomplete applications or those in any other format will not be accepted. Applications received after the last date will not be accepted. One candidate can submit only one application. This engagement is purely temporary and alternative; no claim for regularization or permanency will be entertained in future. Selected teachers will initially be engaged for a maximum of 6 months, extendable by another 6 months on satisfactory service, subject to a maximum total tenure of 1 year, or until permanent teachers are posted by the department, whichever is earlier. No leave or transfer benefits will be admissible; no residential facility will be provided at the school. Service can be terminated immediately without prior notice for violation of agreement terms or unsatisfactory work.

faq.question: Q1. What is Dumka Ghanti Adharit Shikshak Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: It is a recruitment drive by the District Welfare Office, Dumka, to hire 83 hourly/bell-based teachers on a temporary Service Procurement basis for ST/Backward Class residential schools in Dumka district.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Dumka Ghanti Adharit Shikshak Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application is 25-07-2026, up to 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application start date?
faq.answer: Applications can be submitted from 04-07-2026 onward.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are there in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 83 total vacancies across primary and subject-teacher categories.

faq.question: Q5. What is the mode of application?
faq.answer: The application mode is offline. Candidates must submit their forms in a sealed envelope to the Project Director, ITDA (Welfare Branch), Dumka.

faq.question: Q6. Who is eligible to apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Only candidates who are domiciles of Dumka district, meeting the post-wise educational qualification and age criteria, are eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q7. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 21 years and the maximum age is 55 years, calculated as on 31-05-2026.

faq.question: Q8. Is there an application fee for Dumka Ghanti Adharit Shikshak Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Not Announced Yet. The official notification does not specify any application fee.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary for these teacher posts?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be paid Rs. 200 per teaching period, up to a maximum of 4 periods per working day.

faq.question: Q10. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is merit-based, done by a district-level committee, with preference to block-level and local candidates, following the Jharkhand district reservation roster.
